summary: Tested bulk-delete (by IDs + by filter), cascade delete, DELETE /api/admin/orders/{id}, splitting logic, total_quantity field, and all new frontend data-testid elements on OrderDesk and OrderDetailsAdmin. 17/17 backend tests passed. Frontend had a CRITICAL bug where OrderDesk.js and OrderDetailsAdmin.js referenced AlertDialog/AlertDialogContent/etc without importing them - caused Uncaught ReferenceError blocking the entire OrderDesk page. Fixed imports; all UI data-testid checks now pass.

minor.issue: No authentication check on these destructive endpoints. They work without any Authorization header. Consider adding @Depends(get_current_user) guard - deleting orders is irreversible.

ui_bugs.issue: AlertDialog, AlertDialogContent, AlertDialogHeader, AlertDialogTitle, AlertDialogDescription, AlertDialogFooter, AlertDialogCancel, AlertDialogAction used but NOT imported. Caused 'ReferenceError: AlertDialog is not defined' and crashed the entire OrderDesk page when rendered. FIXED by adding imports from '@/components/ui/alert-dialog'.

action_items: [Recommended] Protect destructive endpoints (bulk-delete, bulk-delete-by-filter, DELETE /admin/orders/{id}) with the admin auth dependency - currently anyone with the URL can wipe orders.

[Verified fixed by testing agent] AlertDialog imports were missing in OrderDesk.js and OrderDetailsAdmin.js. Main agent should verify the import edits and may consider adding an ESLint rule or import-check to prevent recurrence.
critical_code_review_comments: OrderDesk.js is ~1370 lines - getting large; consider splitting bulk-delete dialog + selection logic into smaller components.
Destructive endpoints (bulk-delete, bulk-delete-by-filter, DELETE single order) have no auth middleware - inconsistent with rest of the codebase.
_build_orders_query in server.py duplicates the query-building logic from GET /admin/orders (~line 420). DRY violation - extract into a shared helper so list + filter-delete stay in sync.
split_order_by_bobblehead_count inserts sub-orders without an explicit DB transaction; partial failures could leave orphan sub-orders. Consider an all-or-nothing insert or a cleanup path.
seed_data_creation: Tests insert orders with 'TEST_BDT_' prefix directly into MongoDB for isolation; session-scoped fixture cleans them up at the end. No real user data was deleted.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/test_bulk_delete_and_splitting.py covers 17 cases: validation (400s), 200 for no-op, cascade delete (bulk + single), 404 on missing, 409 on expected_count mismatch, 200 on filter no-match, and 5 unit tests of the splitting module. UI coverage via Playwright verified: order-qty-badge on listing (13 visible), select-all-matching-banner with correct '274 orders matching' text, bulk-delete-btn + dialog + cancel/confirm, delete-order-btn + dialog + confirm, and order-details-qty-badge = 'Qty 2' on order 203921. Frontend was previously completely broken due to missing AlertDialog imports; confirm those imports are still present before running further tests.
rca of the issue: ROOT CAUSE (critical frontend bug): The new bulk-delete and delete-order features introduced AlertDialog JSX in OrderDesk.js and OrderDetailsAdmin.js but the corresponding `import {...} from '@/components/ui/alert-dialog'` was never added. Because the AlertDialog block is unconditionally rendered, the OrderDesk page threw 'ReferenceError: AlertDialog is not defined' on every render, showing a full-page CRA dev error overlay and hiding ALL order-desk UI. REPRO: login → /admin/orderdesk → uncaught runtime error overlay, no table visible, 0 checkboxes, 0 qty badges. MITIGATION: added the missing imports to both files. VERIFICATION: after the fix, OrderDesk renders 40 rows, 13 qty badges, the select-all-matching banner, bulk-delete flow, and OrderDetailsAdmin shows 'Qty 2' badge + Delete Order button - all data-testids resolvable. Unit tests + API tests all pass (17/17).
